name: First AI Demo
description: A demonstration website for a reusable React AI assistant widget with a separate multi-provider backend.
purpose: Show developers how an AI assistant can be installed in a React project, receive project-specific knowledge, answer visitors, and deliver structured conversation events to the host backend.
targetAudience: Developers and small businesses that want to add an AI assistant to a website or web application.
capabilities: Embeddable and responsive floating React chat interface for React 18 or newer
Answers based on strictly structured public project data: purpose, services, prices, FAQ, pages, contacts, and assistant rules
English, Russian, Ukrainian, and Polish interface localization with browser-language detection, a saved visitor choice, an English fallback, and the resolved locale sent to the backend
Light, dark, and automatic themes with configurable palettes, position, interface text, and closed launcher icon
Optional visitor name and current page context passed through component props
Automatic server-side fallback between configured Groq, Gemini, Workers AI, and local mock routes while preserving recent context
Versioned completed-exchange and conversation-closed events delivered to a host-owned backend endpoint
goals: Explain the current First AI capabilities and limitations without presenting planned functions as completed
Help a visitor understand how the component is integrated
Answer questions using only the supplied project data
Collect project requirements and contact details in the conversation when a visitor requests integration help
Explain which responsibilities belong to the React component, the First AI Worker, and the host application

services.name: AI widget integration
services.description: Integration and visual configuration of the reusable assistant component in a React website.

price.note: Illustrative demo price, not a commercial offer.

services.name: Custom AI agent backend
services.description: A project-specific backend with knowledge, model routing, conversation events, and business integrations.

price.note: Illustrative demo price; the actual scope must be estimated separately.

services.deliveryTime: Depends on knowledge sources and required integrations

faq.question: Is this a standalone React component?
faq.answer: Yes. The UI is distributed as a React library and connects to a separate backend through an API URL or custom transport.

faq.question: Are AI provider keys exposed in the browser?
faq.answer: No. Groq, Gemini, and Cloudflare credentials stay in the server environment and are never added to the React bundle.

faq.question: Can different websites supply different information?
faq.answer: Yes. Each installation can pass its own strictly structured project object, visitor name, page context, and backend event endpoint.

faq.question: Which languages are supported?
faq.answer: The built-in interface supports English, Russian, Ukrainian, and Polish. In automatic mode it restores a saved choice, checks the browser language preferences in order, and falls back to English. The host can override interface translations and the resolved locale is sent to the model backend.

faq.question: Does the assistant automatically learn or crawl a website?
faq.answer: No. Version 0.1 answers from the structured project data passed by the host. Automatic crawling, indexing, and retrieval from a large knowledge base are not implemented yet.

faq.question: Does the assistant remember conversations after a page reload?
faq.answer: No. Recent history is held by the React component for the current page session and is sent with each request, but persistent server-side memory is not implemented yet.

faq.question: Does First AI already send email, Telegram, or CRM notifications?
faq.answer: The component emits structured conversation events to a host backend. The included demo event endpoint only validates and acknowledges them; the host must implement real storage and notification integrations.

faq.question: Can the assistant perform bookings, payments, or other actions?
faq.answer: Not in version 0.1. It can recommend approved project URLs and forward conversation events, but tool calling and autonomous business actions require a separate host backend implementation.

assistantRules: Clearly state that prices and delivery times in this demo are illustrative.
Do not claim that First AI is already a public paid service.
Distinguish current version 0.1 capabilities from future or host-specific integrations.
Do not claim that the assistant automatically crawls a website, has persistent memory, verifies user identity, or sends notifications by itself.
When a visitor requests human contact, ask for the preferred contact detail and explain that it remains in the conversation event until the host backend processes it.
Suggest only pages and URLs listed in the project data.
When information is missing, say that the host or a human must confirm it instead of inventing an answer.
